DR. JAYANTA KUMAR ROY (JALPAIGURI): Sir, today, I rise to extend my whole-
hearted support to the “Tribhuvan” Sahkari University Bill, 2025 – a visionary and
a timely initiative under the leadership of hon. Home and Cooperation Minister, Shri
Amit Shah ji. This Bill is not just about setting up another university. It is a national
mission to revive, professionalise and modernise India’s cooperative sector, which
is the very backbone of our rural economy and social structure.
Sir, as you are aware, India’s cooperative movement is one of the largest in
the world, with over 8.5 lakh cooperatives and nearly 29 crore members. These
cooperatives serve the poorest and the most marginalised -- our farmers, women,
artisans, fishermen, and workers. But today, they are struggling due to lack of
trained leadership, modern management practices, and technological integration.
Hon. Speaker, Sir, I may bring to your attention a specific case of West
Bengal; a State where the cooperative movement once had a glorious past. But
today, the cooperative sector in West Bengal is a shadow of its former self.
Rampant politicisation and corruption have crippled these institutions. State-
controlled interferences have eroded trust, turning cooperatives into political tools
26.03.2025 1005
rather than engines of social and economic empowerment. Farmers’ credit
societies, which should have been the lifeline of rural development, are now facing
bankruptcy and public distrust. Women’s cooperatives, artisans and weavers are
left without markets or modern training, leading to loss of livelihoods.
Sir, the cooperative spirit that built rural West Bengal has been
systematically destroyed. West Bengal stands as a glaring example of what
happens when cooperatives are not nurtured. Therefore, this Bill is not only
necessary but urgent. By establishing the Tribhuvan Sahkari University as an
institution of national importance, we are sending a strong message that
cooperatives matter and India is serious about reforming and empowering them.
Sir, this university will have multiple crucial roles. It will impart modern,
technical, and professional education in cooperative management, creating a new
cadre of trained cooperative leaders; people who know how to run cooperatives
efficiently, transparently, and profitably. It will promote research and innovation in
the cooperative sector so that we bring Artificial Intelligence, digital finance, and
blockchain to agriculture, dairy, fisheries, and rural enterprises. It will provide
training and capacity building for grassroot members -- be it a small farmer of
Jalpaiguri, a tea garden worker of Dooars, or a fisherman in the Sunderbans -- so
that they can compete in today’s market-driven economy. It will also help in creating
market linkages, promoting exports, and building global standards for the Indian
cooperative products from handicrafts to organic food.
Sir, we must remember that cooperatives are not just about profit. They are
about empowerment, equity, and dignity. They are the soul of Gandhian economics
26.03.2025 1006
and the dream of Gram Swaraj. Today, under the visionary leadership of Prime
Minister Shri Narendra Modi ji and with Amit Shah ji’s dedicated efforts to
rejuvenate the Ministry of Cooperation, we are witnessing the rebirth of this sector
and this university will be its intellectual and professional foundation.
Hon. Speaker, Sir, I also want to emphasize that for a State like West Bengal,
where lakhs of poor farmers and workers have been let down by failed State
policies, this University can train a new generation of cooperative leaders, young
people who are educated, honest, and driven by the cooperative spirit of service.
The Tribhuvan Sahkari University will be the answer to that call, a place
where rural people will learn to stand on their own feet, to manage their affairs, and
to lead their communities towards prosperity.
Hon. Speaker, Sir, let us also remember that in an era where big corporates
dominate markets, only strong, well-managed cooperatives can protect the rights
of farmers, artisans, and rural producers. If we want ‘Sabka Saath’, ‘Sabka Vikas’,
‘Sabka Vishwas’, and ‘Sabka Prayas’, we must empower cooperatives and this Bill
is a step towards that direction.
Sir, Shah Ji’s vision of a Sahakari Bill is not a dream, it is necessary for
inclusive self-reliant India and I believe this university will serve as the IIM, the IIT
and the AIIMS of the cooperative sector giving it professional strength, academic
rigour and technological edge.
In conclusion, hon. Speaker, I urge this House to pass this historic Bill
unanimously and send a message to the people of India that cooperatives are the
future and India stands with its farmers, women and workers. Let this University be
26.03.2025 1007
the lighthouse of a new cooperative revolution, transparent, professional and
people-driven.
Thank you, Sir.

PROF. SOUGATA RAY (DUM DUM): Sir, my amendment No. 1 is for Tribhuvan
Sahkari University. This should be named as ‘National Cooperative University’.
Tribhuvan Das Patel was not the only man doing cooperative in this country, and
the Home Minister has not mentioned Dr. Kurien’s name. So, I move the
amendment.
I beg to move:
“Page 3, line 36, -
for ‘“Tribhuvan” Sahkari University’
substitute ‘National Cooperative University’.” (1)
Page 3, for lines 41 and 42, -
substitute “Gujarat and an Eastern Campus of the
University at Kolkata in the State of West
Bengal. The University may also establish
Schools or outlying campuses or affiliate
institutes or sub-centres at all state capitals
and at such other places in India as it may
deem fit:”.” (2)
माननीय अ(cid:197)य(cid:177) : माननीय गहृ म(cid:253)ं ी जी, (cid:179)या आप कुछ बोलना चाहते ह(cid:167)?

(cid:174)ी अिमत शाह : मा(cid:198)यवर, ये िजतने माननीय सद(cid:214)य कु(cid:229)रयन साहब का नाम लेते ह(cid:167), उन सबको यह

INTERNET
The Original Version of Lok Sabha proceedings is available on Parliament of India Website at the
following address:
www.sansad.in/ls
LIVE TELECAST OF PROCEEDINGS OF LOK SABHA
Lok Sabha proceedings are being telecast live on Sansad T.V. Channel. Live telecast begins at 11
A.M. everyday the Lok Sabha sits, till the adjournment of the House.
